ops.title: cc-guides renders push via per-repo deploy keys (CC_GUIDES_DEPLOY_KEY)
ops.body:
**Decision (2026-07-19):** fleet guides re-renders push over SSH with a per-repo write deploy key instead of GITHUB_TOKEN.

**Why:** GITHUB_TOKEN can never carry `workflows:write`, so any render delta touching `.github/workflows/**` (e.g. the docs.yml build fragment) was rejected — and GITHUB_TOKEN pushes never retrigger workflows, so even successful render commits silently skipped the docs rebuild.

**Mechanics:**
- `cc-guides/.github/workflows/re-render.yml` (action-v1 ≥ v1.3.0, commit 7c90754): checkout takes `ssh-key: ${{ secrets.CC_GUIDES_DEPLOY_KEY }}`; an absent secret leaves the input empty and checkout falls back to token auth, so un-onboarded repos keep working (minus workflow-delta pushes).
- Each consumer's `guides.yml` re-render job adds `secrets: inherit` (repo-bootstrap stub template updated, plugin 0.56.4).
- Per repo: ed25519 deploy key (title `cc-guides-render`, write access) + `CC_GUIDES_DEPLOY_KEY` actions secret. Onboarded 2026-07-19: captain-hook, cc-transcript, cc-steer, docker-dsl, experiment-at-home, spawnllm.
- Loop safety: a render push triggers only the caller's `check` job (re-render runs on dispatch/schedule only). Deploy-key pushes DO retrigger CI + Documentation builds — that is desired (render commit → docs rebuild) and new behavior vs token pushes.

**Proven:** dispatched re-renders on all 6 repos; 5 pushed `.github/workflows/docs.yml` deltas green (commits 8f039b87/58957178/912e049b/c31b0c8a/a46174f4), retriggered Documentation builds green.

**Onboarding a new docs repo:** generate key, `gh repo deploy-key add --allow-write`, `gh secret set CC_GUIDES_DEPLOY_KEY`, ensure `secrets: inherit` in guides.yml (scaffolded by repo-bootstrap ≥0.56.4).
